summary: Iter-38 frontend-only verification of 3 enhancements: (1) DishDetailModal on /restaurant — opens via open-detail-{id} on dish image, renders dish-modal-title/description/ingredients (2 chips for Butter Chicken), 3 portion buttons (regular/large/family) with multiplier price math (₹380 → ₹760 large → ₹1520 family), dish-modal-add adds 2 copies for Large then closes (qty-{id}=2 confirmed), dish-modal-buy redirects anonymous user to /login?next=/restaurant/checkout. Close via X / Escape / backdrop click all verified. (2) Category strip — 6 chips render emoji glyphs inside .cat-emoji-3d at font-size 30px with chunky drop-shadow filter (All=🍽️, Mains=🍛, Starters=🥟, Tiffin Specials=🍱, Desserts=🍰, Beverages=🥤). (3) /login halo — exactly 8 [data-testid=bad-stuff-pill-0..7] circular pills positioned around the centre with correct labels (Ajinomoto, Maida, Artificial Flavours, Artificial Colours, Polished Grains, Refined Oil, Palm Oil, Pre-made Gravy), each carrying .bad-stuff-pill-zero='0%' + .bad-stuff-pill-label, parent .bad-stuff-bg has aria-hidden=true and pointer-events:none. OTP login flow end-to-end still works (phone fill → send-otp → otp-input appears → verify-otp → redirect to /restaurant with 'Welcome to efoodcare' toast). All 13 assertions across both pages PASSED.

integration_issues.issue: Total shown in modal (₹760/₹1520) does not match actual checkout — buyNow sessionStorage payload hardcodes qty:1, ignoring selected portion. User pays for 1 portion despite modal Total. Cart is also inflated as side-effect.

action_items: Fix DishDetailModal Buy-now portion mismatch: pass portion qty into Restaurant.buyNow so /restaurant/checkout?buynow=1 charges for portions, not 1. Currently sessionStorage hardcodes qty:1 — Large/Family buyers will be undercharged & cart is also silently inflated. See critical_code_review_comments[0].
critical_code_review_comments: DishDetailModal Buy-now does NOT honour the portion selector. Restaurant.buyNow() sets sessionStorage `efc_buynow_v1 = {[id]: {id, qty: 1}}` (hardcoded qty:1) — so a user who selects Large (2 portions) or Family (4 portions) and clicks Buy will be charged for only 1 portion at /restaurant/checkout?buynow=1, even though the modal showed Total=₹760 / ₹1520. Meanwhile DishDetailModal.handleBuy pre-adds 'portions' copies to the main cart before navigating, so the cart is silently inflated to 2/4 items but the actual checkout via buynow=1 ignores them. Fix options: (a) Pass portion count into buyNow(it, qty) so sessionStorage carries the correct qty; (b) Drop the buynow=1 sessionStorage shortcut when modal context is present and instead let checkout use cart contents. Either way the Total figure shown in the modal must match what user pays. Severity: HIGH for Large/Family buyers.

DishDetailModal: deriveIngredients() falls back to only ['Filter / cold-pressed oil'] for items with no keyword match — but Butter Chicken got 2 hints (cream-match + brand promise). Items like generic 'Chicken 65' may surface a thin ingredient list (often just 1 chip) — acceptable but worth a richer keyword set.

CategoryStrip imports Lucide icons (Sandwich/Soup/etc.) just for the legacy CATEGORY_ICON export; bundle still ships unused icon variants. Minor — consider tree-shaking by moving CATEGORY_ICON to its own file.

BadStuffBackground positions pills at fixed top/left percentages of the parent .bad-stuff-bg — the parent likely covers the viewport, so on tall screens pills sit at the screen edges far from the login card. Visually they 'ring the page', not the form. Confirm intent vs. anchoring the halo container to the form card itself.
Mocked: razorpay-mock, msg91-stub (DEV OTP echoed in toast).
test_credentials: OTP DEV mode — phone 9876543210, dev_otp echoed in success toast and in response body.
seed_data_creation: None. Existing menu items used (Butter Chicken main_butter_chicken). Cart state was modified during testing but no DB writes (anonymous session).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Tested on viewport 1280x900. Verified items: open-detail-{id} button on every DishCard, dish-detail-modal portal renders at body root, dish-modal-{title,description,ingredients,portions,total,close,add,buy}, dish-portion-{regular,large,family} (active state has class with primary tint, aria-pressed=true). Portion math = unit_price * portions (regular=1, large=2, family=4). Modal close paths all work: X-button, Escape key, backdrop click. Buy-now for anonymous users redirects to /login?next=%2Frestaurant%2Fcheckout%3Fbuynow%3D1. Category icons: 6 categories, each [data-testid=cat-icon-{slug}] contains .cat-emoji-3d at 30px with multi-layer drop-shadow. Bad-stuff halo: parent .bad-stuff-bg aria-hidden=true, pointer-events=none (login form fully interactive on top). 8 pills evenly distributed (45° apart) at radius 38% from centre — top=12%/50%/88% confirmed. OTP flow: 9876543210 → send-otp → OTP appears in success toast body ('Found OTP in body: 363708') → verify-otp → /restaurant with Welcome toast. Potential issue worth main-agent review: dish-modal-buy double-adds items because both handleBuy() pre-adds 'portions' copies AND Restaurant.buyNow() also calls onAdd internally. For logged-in user with Large selected, this means 4 items added instead of 2 when clicking Buy-now from the modal.
rca of the issue: All visible flows function correctly per the test plan. One latent functional bug found via code review: dish-modal-buy at portion ∈ {Large, Family} silently mismatches Total shown vs. actual checkout. handleBuy() loops onAdd `portions` times (inflating main cart), then calls onBuy(item) which is Restaurant.buyNow — buyNow hardcodes sessionStorage `efc_buynow_v1 = {[id]: {id, qty: 1}}` and navigates to /restaurant/checkout?buynow=1 (which keys off sessionStorage, ignoring the cart). Result: user sees Total ₹760 (Large × ₹380) in modal, but checkout will only line-item 1 × ₹380. Fix: thread portion qty into buyNow signature → `buyNow(it, selected.portions)` and write `qty: portions` into the sessionStorage payload. (For Regular=1 the bug is invisible — that's why the buy-now redirect test still passed.)
